title: cheilanthes odora proles maderensis
intro: Cheilanthes odora proles maderensis, also known as Madeiran lip fern, is a plant species native to the island of Madeira, Portugal. It belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, and its name is derived from its unique combination of the genus Cheilanthes and the species odora proles maderensis, which refers to its distinctive scent. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Cheilanthes odora proles maderensis is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and is prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures.
